title: cochlearia altaica
intro: Cochlearia altaica, also known as Alaskan Rock Cress, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and southwestern Canada. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. In herbal preparations, Alaskan Rock Cress is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and is also added to food products as a spice and garnish.
